stem: What is the maximum possible number of edges in a simple planar graph with \( [[V]] \) vertices (where \( [[V]] \geq 3 \))?
solution: For a simple planar graph with \( V \geq 3 \), the edge count satisfies \( E \leq 3V - 6 \), and this bound is achieved by a triangulation. So the maximum is \( 3([[V]]) - 6 = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to subtract 6 from \( 3V \).
This uses \( 2V - 4 \), the tighter bound that holds only for triangle-free (e.g. bipartite) planar graphs.
This subtracts only 3 instead of 6.
This adds 6 instead of subtracting it.
This counts the edges of the complete graph \( \binom{V}{2} \), which exceeds the planar bound for large \( V \).
This subtracts 12, doubling the correction term.

stem: What is the maximum possible number of edges in a simple planar graph with \( [[V]] \) vertices that contains NO triangles (in particular, this covers every bipartite planar graph)?
solution: A triangle-free simple planar graph satisfies the tighter bound \( E \leq 2V - 4 \) (every face is bounded by at least 4 edges, not just 3, when there are no triangles). So the maximum is \( 2([[V]]) - 4 = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses the general planar bound \( 3V-6 \), which allows triangular faces and so is too loose here.
This forgets to subtract 4 from \( 2V \).
This subtracts only 2 instead of 4.
This drops the factor of 2 on \( V \) entirely.
This is off by one from the correct maximum.
This is off by one from the correct maximum.

stem: A simple planar graph has \( [[E]] \) edges. Using the bound \( E \leq 3V - 6 \), what is the minimum possible number of vertices \( V \) it could have?
solution: The bound \( E \leq 3V - 6 \) rearranges to \( V \geq \dfrac{E+6}{3} \); since \( V \) must be a whole number, the minimum is the ceiling of this quantity: \( \left\lceil \dfrac{[[E]]+6}{3} \right\rceil = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This leaves the bound unrounded; \( V \) must be a whole number, so a non-integer value must be rounded UP.
This subtracts 6 instead of adding it before dividing.
This drops the \( +6 \) term and rounds down instead of up.
This ignores the \( +6 \) correction term entirely.
This is off by one from the correct minimum.
This is off by one from the correct minimum.

stem: A simple graph has \( [[V]] \) vertices and \( [[E]] \) edges, which is MORE than the maximum \( 3V - 6 \) allowed for a simple planar graph on \( [[V]] \) vertices (so this graph cannot be planar). By how many edges does \( E \) exceed the planar bound \( 3V - 6 \)?
solution: The planar bound for \( [[V]] \) vertices is \( 3([[V]]) - 6 \). The excess is the given edge count minus this bound: \( [[E]] - (3 \cdot [[V]] - 6) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to add back the \( -6 \) correction of the bound before subtracting.
This flips the sign on the 6, adding it instead of accounting for the bound's actual subtraction.
This computes the excess with the sign reversed, giving a negative value.
This divides instead of finding the difference from the bound.
This is off by one from the correct excess.
This is off by one from the correct excess.
